title: Basic inheritance in C++

exercises.title: Derive a Student from Person
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Create a base class `Person` and a derived class `Student` that inherits from `Person` using public inheritance. This exercise practices the basic `class Derived : public Base` syntax and calling the base constructor from the derived constructor.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `name` (no spaces, 1-50 characters) and an integer `age` (1 <= age <= 120).
- Line 2: an integer `studentId` (1 <= studentId <= 99999).

## Output

- Line 1: `Name: <name>`
- Line 2: `Age: <age>`
- Line 3: `Student ID: <studentId>`

All values come from calling member functions on a single `Student` object.

## Requirements

- Define a class `Person` with private members `std::string m_name` and `int m_age`. Its constructor takes `std::string_view name` and `int age`.
- `Person` has public const getters `getName()` (returns `std::string_view`) and `getAge()` (returns `int`).
- Define a class `Student` that publicly inherits from `Person`. It adds a private member `int m_studentId`.
- `Student`'s constructor takes `std::string_view name`, `int age`, and `int studentId`. It must delegate to `Person`'s constructor for the name and age.
- `Student` has a public const getter `getStudentId()` (returns `int`).
- In `main`, read input, construct a `Student`, and print the three output lines using only the `Student` object and its inherited + own getters.

exercises.title: Vehicle hierarchy with inherited behavior
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a small vehicle hierarchy where a `Vehicle` base class provides shared state and behavior, and two derived classes (`Car` and `Truck`) each add their own specialized member. This reinforces that derived classes inherit base members and functions while extending the class with new data.

## Input

- Line 1: a string `carMake` (no spaces, 1-50 characters), an integer `carYear` (1900 <= carYear <= 2100), and an integer `numDoors` (2 <= numDoors <= 6).
- Line 2: a string `truckMake` (no spaces, 1-50 characters), an integer `truckYear` (1900 <= truckYear <= 2100), and a double `payloadTons` (0.1 <= payloadTons <= 100.0).

## Output

- Line 1: `Car: <carMake> (<carYear>), <numDoors> doors`
- Line 2: `Truck: <truckMake> (<truckYear>), <payloadTons>t payload`

`payloadTons` is printed with exactly one decimal place.

## Requirements

- Define a class `Vehicle` with private members `std::string m_make` and `int m_year`. Its constructor takes `std::string_view make` and `int year`.
- `Vehicle` has public const getters `getMake()` (returns `std::string_view`) and `getYear()` (returns `int`).
- Define a class `Car` that publicly inherits from `Vehicle`. It adds a private member `int m_numDoors`. Its constructor takes `std::string_view make`, `int year`, and `int numDoors`, delegating to `Vehicle`'s constructor.
- `Car` has a public const getter `getNumDoors()` (returns `int`).
- Define a class `Truck` that publicly inherits from `Vehicle`. It adds a private member `double m_payloadTons`. Its constructor takes `std::string_view make`, `int year`, and `double payloadTons`, delegating to `Vehicle`'s constructor.
- `Truck` has a public const getter `getPayloadTons()` (returns `double`).
- In `main`, read input, construct one `Car` and one `Truck`, and print the output using inherited and own getters. Use `std::fixed` and `std::setprecision(1)` for the payload.

test_cases.label: Common vehicles
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Toyota (2022), 4 doors
Truck: Ford (2020), 5.5t payload

test_cases.label: Sports car and heavy truck
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Porsche (2024), 2 doors
Truck: Volvo (2019), 18.0t payload

test_cases.label: Edge values
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Car: Mini (1960), 2 doors
Truck: Mack (2100), 100.0t payload
